body: TUNGSTEN -- live run + deep diagnosis complete, 5 real fixes shipped. Watched SLS(62) live through all 9 Phase 3B regimes without a hang for the first time ever (WARRANT#31 fix confirmed at the exact regime/draw that killed SLS43 in pass 17). Result: honest null -- all 9 regimes regularized to baseline, Phase 1 itself barely above random. Deep-dived the full log and found real, verified root causes: WARRANT#48 fixed the new relationship-measurement code running silently with zero logging. WARRANT#49 fixed Gate 9s one-sided inversion print (was only checking one tail -- the live trim was always correctly symmetric, so this never affected a real trade, just under-reported). WARRANT#50 added a strictly-additive two-fold walk-forward tightening to Phase 3Bs overfit check (the long-flagged hypothesis #3 from the SLS29/30 session). Then produced a 31-item non-padded problem catalog covering signal weakness, search methodology, sample authenticity, and validation gaps, each mapped to the owning forge agent with a concrete fix -- and implemented the top 2: WARRANT#51 (OptimizeExitHorizonPerRegime had ZERO holdout validation across 1,440 combos/regime -- worse than the already-known Phase 3B gap, and it runs FIRST so Phase 3B inherits its picks) and WARRANT#52 (same function never audited for the terminal-hang risk WARRANT#31 fixed elsewhere). Also self-caught and fixed a stale EA_BUILD_ID from WARRANT#50 (only EA_VERSION was bumped that pass). All 5 fixes compiled clean and diff-verified. Backed up SLS(67) + ledger to x_strategies. Single highest-value remaining item: Phase 3Bs linear optimizer diverges sharply from the live 14-layer enhanced scorer (seen as linear overfit in nearly every regime tonight) -- it tunes weights for a different function than the one that actually trades. That needs careful design, not a blind fix. Full 31-item catalog and detail in forge_ledger.json passes 36-40 and chat.
